title: Mapping the Generative Landscape
concept: Generative models form a family tree: autoencoders branch into VAEs, adversarial training produces GANs, and iterative denoising produces Diffusion models.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Generative AI models?

options.text: Creating brand new content like images, music, or text
Deleting old files to save hard drive space
Connecting your computer to a local printer

exercises.explain: Generative AI is the branch of artificial intelligence focused on creating brand new data—like text, images, or music—rather than just classifying existing data.

cards.title: The Magic of Creation
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how computers generate mind-blowing art from just a few words? It didn't happen overnight! AI image generators evolved through a fascinating family tree of different technologies, each learning to create in its own unique way.

cards.title: Meet the Family Tree
cards.body: Generative AI is a family tree of different techniques. Autoencoders compress and recreate data. Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) add math rules to generate new variations. GANs use a duel between a creator and a detector. Diffusion models build images by slowly cleaning up random, static noise.

cards.title: The Artist Guild
cards.body: Think of AI models like artists with different training. Autoencoders are like students tracing drawings to learn the basics. GANs are like two art students competing and critiquing each other to get better. Diffusion models are like sculptors who start with a messy block of clay and slowly chip away the noise until a beautiful statue appears!

cards.title: Creating a Cosmic Cat
cards.body: When you ask an AI to generate a 'cosmic cat,' a GAN might have a 'Generator' create a cat and a 'Discriminator' check if it looks real. A Diffusion model will start with a screen full of gray static (noise) and slowly refine it until a cat wearing a space helmet emerges.

cards.title: Are All Generators the Same?
cards.body: Many people think all generative AI works the exact same way. In reality, a GAN works like a competition between two networks, while a Diffusion model works like a sculptor slowly carving an image out of random noise. They are completely different branches of the AI family tree!

cards.title: Two Paths to Art
cards.body: Generative models use completely different strategies to create new data. Let's compare the battle-style approach of GANs with the refining approach of Diffusion models.

right.text: A single process starts with pure random noise and gradually cleans it up to form an image.

left.text: Two networks compete: a generator creates fake data, and a discriminator tries to spot the fakes.

exercises.prompt: Which branch of the generative family tree uses a 'creator' network and a 'judge' network competing against each other?
exercises.explain: GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks) set up a game-like competition between a Generator (the creator) and a Discriminator (the judge) to improve image quality.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Autoencoders branch into VAEs, helping AI learn to compress and reconstruct data before generating new things.
exercises.explain: Autoencoders were early models designed primarily to compress data into a smaller representation and then reconstruct it, forming the foundation for later generative models.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how Diffusion models generate artwork.
exercises.sentence: Diffusion models create detailed images by step-by-step removing random _______.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models are famous for starting with random, messy noise and slowly cleaning it up over many steps to reveal a clear image.

exercises.prompt: Which system is using a Diffusion model to generate a new image?

options.text: System A: 'I will create two neural networks and make them fight until the images look real.'
System B: 'I will start with a canvas of random static noise and slowly clean it up step-by-step.'
System C: 'I will search a database of existing cat photos and show you the best match.'

exercises.explain: System B is the AI assistant using a Diffusion model, as it mentions starting with random noise and iteratively cleaning it up. System A uses a GAN, and System C is just search.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a modern image generator like Midjourney or Stable Diffusion. How does a Diffusion model actually create a brand new, clean image?

options.text: They immediately paste random pixels together all at once.
They start with pure noise and slowly clean it up step-by-step.
They copy exact images from their training data without changing them.
They only compress files to make them smaller.

exercises.explain: Diffusion models are defined by their unique two-step process: they destroy an image by adding random noise, and then learn to generate new images by carefully removing that noise step-by-step.
